---
title: "Statistical Analysis"
author: "Ria Rhodes"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
clean <- read.csv("clean.csv")
head(clean)
```

```{r}
mean(clean$X1.Month...Change, na.rm = TRUE)
median(clean$X1.Month...Change, na.rm = TRUE)
sd(clean$X1.Month...Change, na.rm = TRUE)
min(clean$X1.Month...Change, na.rm = TRUE)
max(clean$X1.Month...Change, na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r}
pop <- read.csv("pop.csv")
head(pop)
```

```{r}
states_to_use <- c("Arizona", "Arkansas", "California", 
                   "Maine", "New Jersey", "Tennessee")

pop_sub <- pop |>
  filter(state %in% states_to_use,
         year %in% 2020:2025)

clean_sub <- clean %>%
  filter(state %in% states_to_use,
         Year %in% 2020:2025)
```

```{r}
clean_sub <- clean_sub %>%
  rename(year = Year)
```

```{r}
merged <- pop_sub %>%
  inner_join(clean_sub, by = c("state", "year"))
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

unemp_yearly <- merged %>%
  group_by(state, year) %>%
  summarise(Value = mean(Value, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

```

```{r}
pop_sub$year <- as.integer(pop_sub$year)
unemp_yearly$year <- as.integer(unemp_yearly$year)
pop_density$year <- as.integer(pop_density$year)

final <- pop_density %>%
  filter(state %in% states_to_use,
         year %in% 2020:2025) %>%
  inner_join(unemp_yearly, by = c("state", "year"))
```

```{r}
hist(final$pop_density)
```

```{r}
cor.test(final$pop_density, final$Value, method = "pearson")
cor.test(final$pop_density, final$Value, method = "spearman")
```

```{r}
final %>%
  group_by(state) %>%
  summarise(
    pearson  = cor(pop_density, Value, method = "pearson"),
    spearman = cor(pop_density, Value, method = "spearman")
  )
# Only Cali is positive
# Driven by state differences, not trends :(
  # Denser state, higher unemployment- However, unemployment will fall while density is consistent
)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(final, aes(x = pop_density, y = Value)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "blue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Population Density vs Unemployment",
       x = "Population Density",
       y = "Unemployment Rate")

```

```{r}
final$log_density <- log(final$pop_density)

cor.test(final$log_density, final$Value)
  # Better
```
